synopsis: Virgil B. Roberts, a ruling elder, complained that New River Presbytery erred by not accepting the resignation of Rev. Don Post, Jr. as pastor of Providence Reformed Presbyterian Church, when the minister had tendered his resignation, the congregation had voted to accept it (28-16), and the commissioners appeared before Presbytery. The SJC sustained the complaint, holding that BCO 23-1 requires the Presbytery to accept and dissolve the pastoral relationship when these conditions are met. The Court held that the discretion given to a Presbytery under BCO 23-1 does not give it power to prevent the implementation of a valid decision of a congregation. Vote: 16-3 with dissent.
